title: OpenClaw shell inline-command fallback skipped exec allowlist for single commands
description: OpenClaw evaluates shell-wrapper inline commands against the exec allowlist via resolveInlineChainFallback / evaluateShellWrapperInlineChain. Both functions only route an inline command through the allowlist evaluator when splitCommandChain yields a multi-part chain: they return null whenever the parsed chain has length <= 1. A command request using a single shell inline-command form therefore never receives the expected allowlist decision and is treated as satisfied, so a single inline command can bypass the exec allowlist. This is an incomplete-set-of-cases flaw (CWE-184): the parser branch handles chained inline commands but silently skips the allowlist check for the single-command case.
